channel.title: Lucileee Homepage on Lucileee Homepage
channel.description: Recent content in Lucileee Homepage on Lucileee Homepage

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hi! This is Lucile (Ya-Ting) Yang, a Ph.D. student in Electrical and Computer Engineering, New York University. I got my bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from National Tsing Hua University and my master’s degree in Communication Engineering from National Taiwan University .

Education

  • Ph.D. student in Electrical and Computer Engineering, New York University (2022-now)
  • M.S. student in Graduate Institute of Communication Engineering, National Taiwan University (2020-2022)
    • Master’s Thesis: Game-Theoretic Resource Management for Decomposable DNN Inference in Edge-IoT Framework (Advisor: Prof. Hung-Yu Wei)
  • Exchange student in Computer Technology and Information System, Bilkent University (2019/09-2020/01)
  • B.S. degree of Department of Electrical Engineering, National Tsing Hua University (2016/09-2020/06)

Experience

  • MOST - Intelligent Production Control Exploiting 5G-enabled IIoT and Edge Computing (2020/08-2021/07)
  • EE intern, Logitech (2020/03-2020/06)
  • Industry-Academia Cooperation Project with Yuanta Futures (2019/03-2020/05)
  • Special Topic on Implementation, Vision Science Lab, NTHU (2018/09-2019/09)

Publication

  • Ya-Ting Yang and Hung-Yu Wei, “Edge Computing and Networking Resource Management for Decomposable Deep Learning: An Auction-Based Approach,” APNOMS, Sept. 2021
  • Ya-Ting Yang and Hung-Yu Wei, “Edge-IoT Computing and Networking Resource Allocation for Decomposable Deep Learning Inference,” IEEE Internet of Things Journal, accepted and to appear, 2022
  • Ya-Ting Yang and Hung-Yu Wei, “A Coalition Formation Approach for Privacy and Energy-Aware Split Deep Learning Inference in Edge Camera Network,” IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management, accepted and to appear, 2022
  • Kai-Jung Fu, Ya-Ting Yang, and Hung-Yu Wei, “Split Computing Video Analytics Performance Enhancement with Auction-based Resource Management,” IEEE Access, Volume 10, Page 106495 - 106505, Oct. 2022
  • Ya-Ting Yang, Tao Li, and Quanyan Zhu, “Designing Policies for Truth: Combating Misinformation with Transparency and Information Design.”

Award

  • Honorary Membership of the Society by NTHU, The Phi Tau Phi Scholastic Honor Society of the Republic of China
  • NTHU Student Study Abroad Scholarship, 2019
  • NTHU Academic Excellence Award * 3, 2016-2020

Skill

  • English
    • TOEFL: 101
    • TOEIC: 865
  • Programming
    • C/C++, HTML/CSS, Python (including PyTorch and OpenCV), Java, Verilog

Extracurricular Activities and Leadership

  • Community Services
    • As a reviewer for IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics (2022)
    • As a reviewer for IEEE Internet of Things Journal (2023)
  • Volunteer
    • Bal Ashram, Rajasthan, India (2019 summer)
    • Xin Jiang, China (2018 summer)
  • Student Association
    • NTHUEE Student Association – Activities Department
    • NTHU Artmay 6th – Head of the Publicity Department
    • NTHU EE Camp (2017) – Education Department
    • NTHU EECC Camp (2017, 2018) – Activities Department
